item.title: Bitcoin Retreats From Six-Week High as Iran Conflict Escalates
item.description: Bitcoin retreated from a six-week high after a broader risk-off move swept across global financial markets amid growing concern of an escalation in the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: How Brazil Can Chip Away at China’s Rare Earths Dominance
item.description: When China leveraged its dominance in rare earths to retaliate against US tariffs last year, the disruption to factory operations in America and Europe underscored the vulnerability of Western companies’ supply chains.

item.title: UK Urged to Ban Crypto Donations as US and Others Seek Influence
item.description: British lawmakers are pushing the government to legislate against crypto currency donations to political parties, amid growing concerns about the influence of foreign states including the US.

item.title: MFS Owner Raja Hit With Worldwide Asset Freeze After Collapse
item.description: Officials overseeing the wind-down of Market Financial Solutions Ltd., the UK mortgage lender that borrowed more than £2 billion ($2.7 billion) from Wall Street backers, have obtained a worldwide asset-freezing order against owner Paresh Raja.

item.title: Bank of Canada Holds Rates, Looks Through Near-Term Oil Risk
item.description: The Bank of Canada held interest rates steady, saying it would “look through” the Middle East war’s immediate inflation impact as it kept focus on downside growth risks.

item.title: Meta to Open NYC Retail Flagship on Fifth Avenue
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is opening its first flagship store in Manhattan, expanding its push into physical retail with a space along one of the world’s priciest shopping corridors.

item.title: Bond Traders Lose Faith in Fed Rate Cut This Year on Oil, PPI
item.description: Bond traders priced in lower odds of even a single Federal Reserve interest-rate cut this year as Middle East warfare put upward pressure on oil prices and a US inflation gauge rose more than expected.

item.title: Credit Fears, Oil Surge Rattle Brazil’s New Corporate Bond Sales
item.description: A series of high-profile corporate busts combined with global market volatility is forcing some Brazilian corporate borrowers to scale back and delay new deals, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: SAP CEO Pushes AI Turnaround With New Teams, Use-Based Pricing
item.description: SAP SE’s Chief Executive Officer Christian Klein is setting up a new unit with hundreds of people to push the adoption of its artificial intelligence features and plans to overhaul how it charges clients as the technology threatens its legacy subscription model.

item.title: Oil Market Underpricing Iran Supply Shock, Carlyle’s Currie Says
item.description: Oil hasn’t fully priced in the supply shock of the US-Israeli war on Iran, said Carlyle Group Inc.’s Jeff Currie, who described the global energy market’s current upheaval as the “mirror image of Covid.”

item.title: BHP’s Next Leader Faces Same Problems in Deal-Obsessed Industry
item.description: BHP Group has picked the continuity candidate for its next boss: A life-long company man who has spent quarter of a century at the biggest miner, honing his skills in iron ore and copper, its most important products.

item.title: Prices Paid to US Producers Increase by More Than Forecast
item.description: US wholesale inflation unexpectedly accelerated in February from a month earlier, reflecting higher costs for goods and services.

item.title: Fluidstack Drops Out of Marquee €10 Billion AI Project in France
item.description: Cloud-computing startup Fluidstack Ltd. has pulled out of a high-profile €10 billion ($11.5 billion) data center project in France as it pivots toward the US after winning larger contracts there, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Stock Futures Extend Rally Ahead of Fed Rate Decision
item.description: US stock futures extend a two-day rally as investors remain cautiously optimistic amid rising energy prices and before the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate decision.

item.title: Japan’s Exports to the US Drop 8%; Takaichi and Trump to Meet Thursday in Washington
item.description: Takaichi faces a diplomatic balancing act after Trump called on Japan to deploy warships to help secure the war-stricken Strait of Hormuz — before abruptly dropping the demand.

item.title: School Bus Unicorn Zum Hits $333 Million in Revenue
item.description: Zum Services Inc., a tech company that provides bus and transit technology to more than 4,000 schools, says it hit $333 million in revenue for 2025, up 35% from the year before.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CF Industries, Coupang, Duolingo, Swarmer
item.description: US equity-index futures pared earlier gains after Iranian state TV said part of the giant South Pars gas field was hit in an airstrike. S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.4% at 7:45 a.m. in New York.

item.title: Chile Economic Growth Beat Forecasts at End-2025 as Mining Rose
item.description: Chile’s economy expanded more than expected in the fourth quarter, helped by growth from the crucial mining sector and expectations for market-friendly government policies under President José Antonio Kast.

item.title: Iran War Poses Risk of ‘Major’ Economic Downturn, Singapore President Says
item.description: A prolonged conflict in the Middle East will heighten the risk of a “major economic downturn” and increased inflation, adding a new stress point in financial markets that were already under strain, Singapore’s President Tharman Shanmugaratnam wrote in a Facebook post.

item.title: AI Security Startup Xbow Valued at More Than $1 Billion
item.description: Xbow, a startup that builds AI software to probe applications for security vulnerabilities, has raised a new round of funding that values it at more than $1 billion, a sign of investor enthusiasm for using artificial intelligence in cybersecurity.

item.title: TDR, I Squared Said to Seek Pitches for US IPO of Aggreko
item.description: TDR Capital and I Squared Capital are ramping up preparations for a New York listing of Aggreko Plc, one of the world’s biggest power generator rental firms for events like Glastonbury Festival, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Diesel Squeeze Threatens to Ripple Through Economy
item.description: Prices for the fuel have surged faster than most other petroleum-based products.

item.title: Investors Are Wondering If Stocks Have Seen the Worst of the War
item.description: It’s been a volatile stretch for US equities as Wall Street tries to wrap its arms around the war in Iran. But with the fighting now in its third week, investors are becoming more sanguine about the stock market as signs emerge that the worst may be over.

item.title: Tencent Says It Faces Fewer US Regulatory Risks Than TikTok
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. said it faces fewer risks than TikTok in the US as the Trump administration investigates potential security risks involving the Chinese gaming leader’s data practices.

item.title: Prediction Markets Get Tangled In the Iran War
item.description: Reporter threatened by Polymarket users who bet on the attacks

item.title: Trump Calls for Emergency Fed Cut While His Economist Says All’s Well
item.description: US central bank officials are widely seen holding rates as they assess the implications of higher gas prices

item.title: Sweden Is Set for Wait-and-See Stance on Rate
item.description: Sweden’s central bank is set to confirm that war-induced inflation risks have derailed any prospect of an imminent interest-rate cut, when its latest decision is revealed this week.

item.title: Global Bonds on Best Streak Since War Started as Oil Swings Ease
item.description: Bonds around the world are rallying this week, recovering from a big hit since US-Israeli strikes on Iran began, as oil prices calm down.

item.title: Agriculture Firm ETG Tests Demand for Bond Amid Market Turmoil
item.description: ETG Group, which operates agricultural businesses across Africa, is sounding out investors for its first international bond sale, testing demand at a tumultuous time for the sector.

item.title: South Africa Finance Cop Warns Delistings Risk Market Depth
item.description: South Africa’s financial regulator warned about the decrease in local listings, saying this raises questions about the depth and vibrancy of public capital markets and about how they can support economic growth.
